I wish GMurphy didn't come with such a stupid ability to trigger sacrifice. It is an unremovable condition that can be stacked at massive numbers with normal damage. The trigger card is only 3 cost for some reason. At least make it expensive.

You could kill everyone within 3 turns

Now you have kath ramona
Pollux ramona
I like Traphase, but I get tilted because I try dumb homebrew instead of meta and lose 9/10 times
the classic mistake
Even if you play meta and play perfectly
You get shafted by some random BS luck
True
They full draw wheel turn 1 or 2
You draw wheel starting at turn 4
You perfect play, they just karen rng debuff and counter you
I remember back when they were switching systems for exp, I dropped 1000+ MMR due to bad luck giving me a string of losses
Cassiah rng cleanse 5 times in a row
Faros perfect rng with posse that full revive team or stuff like pickman that hit AoE dmg x3
The next day. I climbed to 3000 with the same deck
The sad thing is, there are interesting builds that I'd like to try, but the game feels a bit too competitive for meme strats
The faros thing happened to me just two games ago
I killed two and left the remaining ones at low health
They get the time warp and undo it instantly
<:24_Clown:1468259444423921871> so skill based gameplay
In fairness, I suppose some builds could enable you to target damage more effectively to prioritise Faros, but I had a Jenkins build and prayged
In a sense, I put myself at RNGesus's mercy
I think that there is skill expression and strategy, but at the height of it the game becomes less fun for card games
Well, off to homebrew and get tilted o7
I wouldn't have much problem with skill based game tbh
It's just toxic af when you try to come up with ideas of comp or different ways to destroy the enemy's comp
Only to find out you lose because of rng
What's the point of playing well if you'll lose anyway
Sometimes it do be like that
Only so much you can do, after all
